title: Volta Acquires Photonics Firm LuminCore in $180M Deal
body:
Volta Systems said Tuesday it has agreed to acquire LuminCore Technologies, a Eindhoven-based maker of photonic interconnect chips, for approximately $180 million in cash and stock. The transaction is expected to close in the second quarter, subject to regulatory approval.

Volta said the deal is driven by a need to control more of its own hardware stack. LuminCore's chips are used to move data between quantum processing units at speeds that conventional copper interconnects cannot match. Currently, Volta sources those components from three vendors; the acquisition would bring production in-house.

LuminCore was founded in 2021 by former ASML engineers and had raised just over $40 million in venture funding before the deal. Its 60-person team will join Volta's hardware division, which is now led by Dr. Elena Vasquez following her move from IBM Research last month.

Volta CEO Rolf Andersen described LuminCore as "a critical piece of plumbing" for Volta's next-generation processor roadmap. Terms of the earnout structure were not disclosed.
